did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The study evaluated the diagnostic performance of the FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer, a computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) system that uses automated image analysis to assess pigmented skin lesions for cutaneous melanoma. The analyzer scores were obtained and compared against histopathology results.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The study is an original experimental investigation with a retrospective design, analyzing histopathologies of 1,076 pigmented skin lesions to evaluate the diagnostic performance (sensitivity, specificity, AUC) of the FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer against histopathology results.

title: Performance of a computer-aided digital dermoscopic image analyzer for melanoma detection in 1,076 pigmented skin lesion biopsies
journal: Journal of the American Academy of Dermatology
brief_summary: This retrospective study evaluated the diagnostic performance of the FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer, a computer-aided digital dermoscopic image analyzer, for detecting cutaneous melanoma in pigmented skin lesions. The researchers analyzed histopathology results from 1,076 biopsied lesions out of 2,500 imaged lesions to assess the system's accuracy. The analyzer produced scores ranging from 0 to 1.00, which were compared against histopathological diagnoses of benign versus malignant lesions. At an optimized cutoff score of 0.50, the system achieved a sensitivity of 56% and a specificity of 74%. The area under the receiver operating characteristics curve was 0.698, indicating poor overall accuracy for melanoma detection. The authors concluded that the device has low sensitivity and emphasized the need for physicians to apply keen clinical judgment when using such tools.

lesion_types_studied.details: The primary target for detection by the FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer; histopathology confirmed malignant lesions were categorized as cutaneous melanoma.
Histopathology confirmed benign lesions served as the comparator group to malignant lesions (melanoma) in the diagnostic performance analysis.

all_task_types.details: The FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer produces a score ranging from 0 to 1.00 to assess pigmented skin lesions for cutaneous melanoma. The study evaluates diagnostic performance (sensitivity, specificity, AUC) by comparing these analyzer scores against histopathology results, which categorize lesions as benign or malignant. The system uses a cutoff score of 0.50 to classify lesions.

datasets_used.name: FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer Dataset (Single Institution Retrospective Cohort)
datasets_used.description: A retrospective cohort of pigmented skin lesions (PSLs) imaged using the FotoFinder digital dermoscopic system at a single institution over a 15-year period. The dataset consists of 2,500 imaged lesions, of which 1,076 were biopsied and had histopathology results used for validation.

use.testing: The 1,076 biopsied lesions with histopathology results were used to evaluate the diagnostic performance (sensitivity, specificity, AUC) of the FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer scores.
use.other: The remaining 1,424 imaged lesions (2,500 total - 1,076 biopsied) were part of the total imaged cohort but were not included in the diagnostic accuracy analysis due to lack of histopathology confirmation.

metrics_used_to_evaluate_results: area under the receiver operating characteristics curve (AUC)

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. It reported descriptive diagnostic performance metrics (sensitivity, specificity, AUC) without statistical inference.

metric: Area under the receiver operating characteristics curve (AUC)
FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer Dataset (1,076.notes: Results were obtained at an optimized cutoff score of 0.50. The study concluded that the analyzer has low sensitivity and poor accuracy as a diagnostic tool for cutaneous melanoma.

architecture_details.description: The paper evaluates a commercially available system, the FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer, which performs automated image analysis of digital dermoscopic images. The specific internal AI architecture, model family, or algorithmic details are not disclosed in the text.

training_details.details: The paper does not report training details, hyperparameters, or parameter counts for the Mole-Analyzer as it is a proprietary commercial device.

classification_or_output_head.output_type: Continuous score (0-1.00) used for binary classification

conclusions: The FotoFinder Mole-Analyzer demonstrated low sensitivity (56%) and poor overall accuracy (AUC 0.698) for detecting cutaneous melanoma in pigmented skin lesions.
Physicians must apply keen clinical judgment when using computer-aided digital dermoscopic image analyzers for screening suspicious pigmented skin lesions.
important_remarks: The study has a retrospective design and involves only a single institution, limiting generalizability.
The analyzer demonstrated poor overall accuracy with an AUC of 0.698.
The system exhibited low sensitivity (56%) for detecting cutaneous melanoma at the optimized cutoff score of 0.50.
The study highlights the unclear clinical utility of digital dermoscopic image analysis despite its popularity.
Unbiased, high-powered studies investigating the efficacy of commercially available CAD systems are limited.
Physicians must apply keen clinical judgment when using such devices for screening suspicious pigmented skin lesions.
